channel.title: SevenAtSevenSection1
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenSection1, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Blizzard conditions and high winds forecast for NYC, East coast
item.description: The winter storm is expected to bring blizzard conditions and possibly up to 2 feet of snow in New York City.

item.title: Vietnam Veterans sue to block proposed 'Independence Arch' near Arlington National Cemetery
item.description: The lawsuit challenges President Trump's plans for "Independence Arch," a 250-foot structure proposed for Memorial Circle.

item.title: Mississippi health system shuts down clinics statewide after ransomware attack
item.description: The attack was launched on Thursday and prompted hospital officials to close all of its 35 clinics across the state.

item.title: Norway's Johannes Klæbo is new Winter Olympics king
item.description: Johannes Klaebo won all six cross-country skiing events at this year's Winter Olympics, the surpassing Eric Heiden's five golds in 1980.

item.title: Trump to raise global tariffs to 15%
item.description: President Trump previously said he would implement 10% global tariffs after the U.S. Supreme Court struck down his tariff policies.

item.title: 7 days until ticket prices rise for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
item.description: Lowest ticket prices to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 end February 27. Up to $680 off individual passes and up to 30% off group passes. Register before they...

item.title: OpenAI debated calling police about suspected Canadian shooter’s chats
item.description: Jesse Van Rootselaar's descriptions of gun violence were flagged by tools that monitor ChatGPT for misuse.

item.title: Microsoft’s new gaming CEO vows not to flood the ecosystem with ‘endless AI slop’
item.description: Is Microsoft's gaming division doubling down on AI?

item.title: NASA moon rocket hit by new problem expected to bump astronauts’ lunar trip into April
item.description: NASA’s new moon rocket has suffered another setback, almost certain to bump astronauts’ lunar trip into spring.

item.title: Wikipedia blacklists Archive.today after alleged DDoS attack
item.description: Wikipedia editors have decided to remove all links to Archive.today, a web archiving service that they said has been linked to more than 695,000 times...

item.title: Google VP warns that two types of AI startups may not survive
item.description: As generative AI evolves, a Google VP warns that LLM wrappers and AI aggregators face mounting pressure, with shrinking margins and limited differenti...

item.title: Blizzard warnings issued for New York City, New Jersey, Connecticut as storm threatens East Coast
The Biggest Homebuyer Discounts in Over 12 Years | Feb. 2026 Update
item.description: Homebuyers are getting the biggest discounts on properties in over 12 years—and it’s only getting started. At this point, nobody can refute that a ful...

item.title: America’s Most Underwater Housing Markets Present a Golden Opportunity For Investors
item.description: The combination of foreclosures and falling housing prices is like throwing chum into the water for a group of hungry sharks eager for deals. In some ...

item.title: Supreme Court strikes down Trump’s sweeping tariffs, sparking fierce pushback and vow of new levies
Should I Use My Home Equity to Buy My Next Rental Property? (Rookie Reply)
item.description: Should you use your home equity to buy a rental property? Whether it’s your primary residence or another investment property, this strategy could help...

item.title: Off by Nearly 1 MILLION Jobs? Why New Jobs Report Will Impact Rentals
item.description: Big economic news dropped last week: labor data, inflation rates, and huge jobs revisions. All of these are already impacting the housing market, but ...

item.title: How the Wealthiest Have Programmed Their Portfolios This Year
item.description: Despite stock markets hovering around record highs, investors are feeling jittery. You can see it in consumer confidence collapsing to its lowest leve...
